Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to produce a Python dictionary mapping activity pairs as tuples to a tuple average time standard deviation We are to estimate these based on typical supply chain process for manufacturing hightech electronics

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to include a representative subset of activity pairs considering pairs of activities that may be separated by multiple steps i e not only direct successors but also eventually following

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: the supply chain SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS So any later activity can follow any earlier after some steps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We need to assign average time and std devs for each pair based on typical durations and variability

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Lets assign each step a mean time and maybe a coefficient of variation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We can define base times for each activity

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: But these are just durations of each activity itself not the time between activities

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For simplicity we could set average time between each pair equal to the mean time of the second activity and standard deviation equal to some fraction for example 20 percent of mean to reflect variability

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given that the prompt gave an example This is just an illustration we need to produce our own for the supply chain activities

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will define a function to compute average time as sum of mean durations of both activities For pair X Y avg mean time X plus mean time Y std sqrt of std time X squared plus std time Y squared

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We need assign std times for each activity as well Lets assign std times maybe 20 percent of mean

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now produce dictionary with these pairs as keys tuples and values as mean std in seconds

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to ensure we have values for each we computed many duplicates we can just keep one per pair
